parts.answer: Using your own words, explain what the girl felt about walking in the meadow (paragraph 5, ‘I was, at that time ...’). • she thought it was private land / they should not be walking / didn ’ t know it was common land • she was excited at doing something new • she was thrilled at the thought of trespassing • she had no idea she would not go there again (with her mother) 1 mark for any of the details to a total of 2. Verbatim lift ‘ the sense of...didn ’ t know this ’ = 0 Re-read paragraph 2 (‘The crunch ...’) and paragraph 4 (‘She remembers ...’). Using your own words, explain what the writer means by the words underlined in each of the following phrases: 1 ‘When the girl stops and listens closely, the rustle of barley in the fields and the buzz of an occasional bee add detail to the texture of the afternoon.’ (lines 9–11) 2 ‘In the middle sits the old school. Still white from its initial coat of paint, it’s a standard one-room school building.’ (lines 13–14) 3 ‘...more of an outcrop of untouched prairie than the other man-made rock piles dotted randomly through the fields.’ (lines 21–22) Examiners should observe the following principles when assessing candidates ’ responses to this question: Part (i) requires an explanation of the meaning of the italicised word or short phrase only. feeling / atmosphere first / beginning / original without a plan / disorderly 1 mark for each explanation in own words, up to a maximum of 3. Note that the definitions contain the essence of an answer. Be careful not to credit a word actually used in the quoted phrase. However, accept that candidates may respond in different ways, e.g. at greater length. 0500/13 May/June 2018 Notes Explain how the writer conveys the quiet and emptiness of the surroundings through the use of language in each of the three underlined words in Question 1(g)(i). You should refer to the whole phrase in your answer and not just the words underlined. the natural / calm sounds that she hears add to the whole peac

Using your own words, explain why the girl ‘has no idea where she is’ (paragraph 1, ‘An eleven-year-old ...’). • she is alone • she is only 11 • she’s miles from home/in the wilderness Award 1 mark for each point to a maximum of 2

State one sound the girl hears before she stops and one sound she hears when she listens more closely (paragraph 2, ‘The crunch of bicycle ...’). • crunch of (wheels on) gravel / roar of a truck (on the highway) / the wind • rustle of barley / buzz of a bee 1 detail from each section required for 1 mark

What details in paragraph 3 (‘The pale grass ...’) suggest that the schoolyard had been neglected for a long time? • the grass is long / up to the knees • yard is overgrown with / weeds / dandelions shrubs • shrubs form (an overgrown square) around it 1 mark for each point to a maximum of 2

Using your own words, explain why the girl does not walk up the steps to the school building (lines 17–19). • she was afraid of / didn ’ t want to disturb any animals that might be inside [1 mark] • she might find something unpleasant that would spoil the perfect day [1 mark] 1 mark for each point to a maximum of 2 Note: Direct lift of ‘ Who knows...summer ’ s day ’ = 0

Using your own words, explain the change in the girl’s feelings after she picks up the rock (lines 25–27). • she admires its appearance • she fears the huge ants Award 1 mark for each point. Don ’ t award a mark for ‘ disgust ’ . 0500/13 May/June 2018 Notes

parts.answer: What do you learn from Passage B about the life of Mary Kingsley and her achievements? Write your answers using short notes. You do not need to use your own words. Up to 10 marks are available for the content of your answer. Information about the life of Mary Kingsley and her achievements: born in England / in 1862 from a young age had to look after her invalid mother / run the house (as mother was ill / father was away for long periods of time) had little formal education read a lot / very interested in tales of voyages / foreign travel in 1891 she became the carer for both of her parents (until they had both died in 1892) she remained single / never married had a substantial income / £500 pa / independent woman decided to travel to West Africa (at the age of 30) studied the region in detail before travelling travelled as a single woman she travelled through wild and dangerous terrain she climbed to the top of Mount Cameroon she conducted scientific research (unlike other women of her time) in particular she studied the culture of the African peoples / recorded large amounts of geographical information about a little-known area collected samples of local flora and fauna a pioneer / she was one of the first women to study geography / paved way for women to study geography 0500/13 May/June 2018 Notes

Now use your notes to write a summary of what Passage B tells you about the life of Mary Kingsley and her achievements. You must use continuous writing (not note form) and use your own words as far as possible. Your summary should include all 10 of your points in Question 3(a) and must be 100 to 150 words. Up to 5 marks are available for the quality of your writing.
